Kind of a weird one for me, cause RL2 is objectively good. Movement+Platforming and combat is snappy, runs great with a consistent and appealing art style. All the different playable characters are fun and dynamic, as well as unique to each other. If that's all you need, this is an easy recommend but the devil is in the details. First things first, this isn't a roguelike. It's not even really a roguelite. You don't make a build over the course of a run with unique item synergies. You choose a procedural generated class based character at the beginning and pick up passive buffs along the way, with options to replace (not augment, not buff) your active skills occasionally popping up. Those passive skills you pick up also have a cost in the form of resolve where if you spend too much resolve to pick up perks, you start to lose TONS of max health. Eventually meta-progression slows up and you'll have to grind for gold to get back to unlocking new fun stuff to play with. By the far the biggest thing that makes this one hard to compete with its contemporaries is that there's no story. There is one but it is entirely optional and entirely unengaging. Pretty quickly you'll find a "question mark" room hoping for a secret only to get dissapointed when it's another wall of text with as much flavor as boiled chicken. So yeah, I prefer the first Rogue Legacy as it just benefits from being more bite sized and focused. Even if that means it's not as deep

I really liked the first half of this. Up until around the third boss I would have happily recommended it. Then it became more and more of a grind, until I realized I was just picking up the controller out of obligation. The early game feels really good. Every run you're earning upgrades, unlocking new things, getting new movement tech, etc. There's a constant feeling of progress. Until there isn't. The problem is, most of those upgrades are meaningless. Healing is rare, so I was really excited to find a lifesteal rune. Heals you when you kill an enemy. Great! Paid an entire run's worth of gold and all of my upgrade materials to craft it. It restores 1 hp. One singular point of HP per enemy killed. I have 400hp. Enemy attacks deal around 50 per hit. But never fear, you can unlock multiple stacking runes! ...By completing obnoxiously hard challenges over and over and over with every class to grind out upgrade materials to unlock the ability to maybe possibly find duplicate blueprints out in the dungeon and then paying exorbitant amounts of resources to craft a second rune. Now you heal 2 hp. Huzzah. In theory I could find a third. I don't know what that would cost. I bought the upgrade that should allow me to find one, but I haven't seen it in 25 hours. And apparently they scale with strength. I say apparently because the scaling is so low that no class and no combination of gear heals more than 1. In NG+38 it might mean something but I don't intend to spend 700 hours getting there. Speaking of gear, let's talk about gear. Gear sucks. You can find blueprints for equipment in the dungeon. You probably can't afford that equipment. It takes money and ore, and while money is plentiful, ore is incredibly stingy, taking many, many runs to afford even one piece. You can then equip it, assuming you've spent ten levels learning how to wear clothes. These give you much needed boosts to your stats. Except they also reduce your Resolve, the stat that lets you equip Relics that you find in the dungeon. Relics can have powerful synergies, except it doesn't matter, because if you equip armor and weapons to have a chance at surviving in the dungeon you won't have the stats to equip the relics you find in the dungeon. It takes a special kind of game to make you not care about finding treasure in a dungeon, but here we are. I stopped in the sixth dungeon. It's the same enemies from the other five dungeons but all their attacks are obnoxious and they have ten times the health so fighting anything is a chore. The eyeballs that used to shoot one fireball now shoot barrages of five in four directions simultaneously and also homing lightning bolts that will track you through walls forever unless you spin kick off of them and in doing so probably launch yourself into the ceiling spikes or invincible saws or barrages of fireballs or void waves or curse ghosts constantly being shot from every direction by enemies that take way too many hits to kill. But the thing that really killed any enthusiasm I originally had was the bosses. Specifically the 5th boss, a bullet hell with way, way, WAY too much health. Because you can't just try again. You can lock the dungeon layout and warp straight to the boss' room (by paying all the spoils of your eventual victory to the architect, hooray), but every. Single. Time. you have to pick a new heir, walk across town, talk to Charon and hope he doesn't have extra random dialogue, take the boat, enter the teleporter, select the boss room, walk to the boss' door in the boss room, sit through the same obnoxiously long intro animation, just to get immediately juggled to death in less time than it took you to get there. And that's assuming you were offered an heir that was even worth making an attempt with. Sometimes you get all bad choices, and the game doesn't let you reroll until you select one and do the entire trek into the dungeon, and then you can retire them and roll three new ones. It's honestly kind of impressive how sharply my enjoyment dropped off, so I guess I can give it that.

One of the best of its kind, and essentially gives you all of the quality of life changes that were lacking in the original. The meta progression is enormous, and there are so many ways to play beyond your initial 'completion'. The legacy system really adds an edge to each run, often hilariously so, while the numbers just keep on getting bigger. It has a God Mode effectively built in via the House Rules system, so you can always progress in some way. So, yeah, it's a metroidvania rogue-ish (I have no confidence in my understanding of the distinction between like and lite) platformer, where you jump, kill and progress - but there is so much fun outside of that loop, that I'd struggle to find anything it lacks. If you loved the first but wanted more, this is it.

Definitely better than the original rogue legacy in many ways, but it becomes incredibly and frustratingly annoying that when you finish doing a full clear of each area over the course of 30+ minutes, you are only be able to buy a few upgrades at best from the skill tree afterwards. And that is also if everything goes to plan, meaning that most of your runs will only be giving you a choice of upgrading 1 measly little stat at the end of it, or if you can even afford to do that. The upgrades available to you at the end of the game only give you a tiny little stat boosts, so you will need to do many 30+ minute runs to build them up to something more useful. I enjoyed it a lot at the beginning, and purposely experimented with all the classes and options available to me. But by the end of it, I found my self re-rolling till I got what I thought was the best class with no negative traits, as it was not worth the extra effort if they had some horrible gimmick tied to them. I was basically given two choices. One, grind out the boss I was stuck on till I perfected my strategy against them, or two, grind out runs until I scraped enough upgrades together till I thought that I could maybe do a little bit more damage against said boss next time, and repeat back to my two choices. The movement in this game is okayish I suppose, just wish I had some other things to work with such a fast fall button so that I don't just watch the projectile fly directly into me and perfectly in my dashing path meaning that I will inevitably be hit by it. And another thing that annoys me is not being able to immediately interrupt my dash as I found my self constantly taking damage because I would attempt to dash away from something too close to an enemy and not be able to stop my self instantly and die as a result. I know a lot of this just boils down to "git gud," but I don't know, just feels like there needed to be something more.  There is a certain part of the game where you have to climb up a tower each and every time you want to try and fight one of the bosses, which took me probably like 10 minutes per attempt. I would be fine with this if getting up the tower wasn't such a pain. You have to perform not-so perfectly timed spin kicks to parkour your way up the tower (oh yeah they got rid of the down sword swipe thing that was in the previous game and replaced it with a much more gimmicky and harder to use spin kick instead, great idea right guys???), which isn't very forgiving as wait too long, and you'll get hit and lose your progress on that section of the parkour. Go too early and you'll miss it, get hit and lose your progress on that section again. Its similar to the cuphead parry mechanic, but the difference is that you have to do it a hell of a lot more compared to cuphead (but im looking at you king dice...).  But this tower also decides to switch it with what you need to do to go up with the other mechanic, dashing. This one is at least a bit more forgiving so its okay. But if you go too high or too low, then you get to watch your self fall downwards as you are only given one dash to work with unless you equip very specific runes or find relics that give you more dashes. And yes, this is another "git gud," moment, but I prefer to at least be given the chance to recover from my mistakes instead of having to complete the entire thing in one smooth motion.  Might I add that there is also enemies in these parkour sections? So then it ends up being either complete the entire thing in 1 smooth motion and dodge the enemy along the way, or get hit by the enemy who'll knock you off, making you teleport back to where you started, and requires you to now wait for the aggro enemy to slowly make its way over to you, so that you can kill it and attempt to complete the parkour again. There is also many parkour sections to go through. And the boss at the top is also significantly harder than any other boss you would have fought so far. This tested my patience greatly. If you could like hold out the spin kick so that it'll instantly be used as you approach something spin-kick-able, then this would of been completely fine with me. There is an accessibility setting where you can turn on flying to completely dodge all of this parkour, but that did not sit right with me using that. But I did abuse relics that gave me flight.  Some classes also are just straight up garbage. A lot of the classes have some gimmick where they can't move around fluently whilst attacking which is just terrible in a game about dodging. This is also just another "git gud," moment but it's not like there is any real incentive to learning to get good at these classes when there is other classes that can move around and just perform better in general. Like the archer for example, can't move while aiming unless, you jump around stupidly then you sorta can, only for your arrow to do the same damage a sword or something else would do. Then there is some magic class that also completely freezes in place for a good second or two and there is no counter play to this apart from just good positioning. And still not even worth it as you will not be constantly in the scenario where you can actually put your self in a good position. I suppose there was some alright classes that are actually workable with, such as the gunslinger and some person with a flying spear thing, but it is still annoying that you lose movement in the game about moving around enemies and projectiles.  I would look past all of these negatives if the gold gain wasn't terrible. They have a system in place where the more upgrades you buy, the more expensive the rest of the upgrades become, which isn't so bad if gold was worth more. You are basically forced to use negative traits to make progress in the skill tree as by the end of my game, they were giving me 400% more gold gain versus the base like 10% gold gain I had on my regular classes with regular traits. And even with this 400% gold gain, the negative traits were such a high skill ceiling that I wouldn't last long enough to make a big enough impact with them. One of the negative traits is a 1 shot, 1 death mechanic which I didn't do too well with. Another one is vegetarian which I can actually work around as its not totally unforgiving (until you break an object and the consumable you are suppose to dodge spawns inside you, instantly damaging you). Then there is one where only crits deal damage. This is extremely annoying to use as some classes can crit easily, whilst others have to go hell and back to get crits. A lot of them involve dashing, a mechanic where I WISH you could just cancel at anytime as I just love dashing into my enemy to try and deal crit damage to them but instead just deal damage to me only instead because I CAN NOT STOP MY SELF FROM DASHING INTO THE ENEMY.  At the end of the game, I was very sick of playing this. The gold grind just became too much, so I just relied on constantly grinding out attempts on the bosses. I killed the second to last boss and that was a mission. But then I was greeted with an even harder boss, so I just threw in the towel and turned enemy health to as low as it could go and enemy damage as low as it could too. I did not want to spend another 10 hours grinding for gold just to kill this guy. And off course there appears to be lots of new game+ content, but I think I will put this down for awhile. If you could increase the gold gain in the accessibility settings to actually fun amounts, then I would be having a lot more fun with this game.  If parts of this review make no sense, it is because I was filled and blinded by rage whilst writing this. I had much more to rant about but I have reached the word limit.  So in conclusion, if you like rogue legacy, you'll like this one for awhile before getting sick of it by the end of it.

Review after watching credits roll!  This is a game I played off and on for years and just started taking it seriously a few weeks ago.  TLDR, when I think of a roguelite game this is the classic definition.  You always start the castle from the beginning, you upgrade all of your skills between runs, and the bosses stay dead until the castle is clear.  Its a simple formula, but I like it.  Yeah, but why would YOU like it in 2025? Frankly the game is charming.  The developers were clearly having a good time.    The "Legacy" aspect of this game is that each time you die in the castle, your character is actually dead.  You have to pick an heir to take up your quest.  All heirs are not created equal.  While one run may have an amazing Ronin with a katana, the next run may be a colorblind bard with IBS.  That has gameplay impacts, you won't see colors and you'll fart a lot.  It helps keep things interesting.  Otherwise the game is responsive and fun to play.  Works great on the steamdeck if you are on the go.  So yeah in conclusion, its simple, fast, and fun to play.  Its a great palette cleanser between bigger games.

Rogue Legacy premier du nom ayant été mon premier rogue lite, je me devais de jouer à celui-ci. Et que dire si ce n'est : "c'est Rogue Legacy, en mieux" ? Malgré l'amour que j'ai pour le style pixel art du premier, ce deuxième opus a aussi une très belle DA avec des animations qui mélangent subtilement la 2D et la 3D. Les contrôles sont fluides et la prise en main est satisfaisante, toujours ce charme de choisir son héritier avec sa classe et ses traits, parfois utiles, parfois amusants, parfois ignobles à jouer... Un vrai plaisir à parcourir, d'autant plus qu'on a ici une véritable histoire qui se déroule au fil de nos parties. D'abord très cryptique, elle donne petit à petit de la profondeur aux lieux, boss et personnages présents. Je recommande sans hésiter aux habitués de rogue lites tout comme à ceux qui voudraient s'y essayer pour la première fois !

This game has a lot of really fun and enjoyable things going for it, unfortunately it commits the cardinal sin of modern game design, it has RNG problems not that make the game too hard to play, but that can make you have to play in non fun ways.  There's a boss, The Void Beast Murmur, which requires you to engage in classic 2D Mario-style platforming challenge where walls of damage with gaps are coming towards you from the right side of the screen and you have to dodge and progress. It randomly creates the pattern of orbs it sends, and one of them is a "full height" wall that you have to hide in one of the limited lowered spaces to avoid. This can spawn extremely frequently. It doesn't cause the platforming to be more challenging, you just run backwards until you get to a safe spot, but it does mean you are at the mercy of a dice roll on whether or not it's *extremely* tedious and boring to get through. When I googled to see if other people had that experience, the advice was "oh just wait until you RNG into one of the mods that makes it not a problem for your character". Again, that sounds tedious and unfun. I don't want to have to wait on additional RNG to hit to unscrew the boss's bad rng and make it an enjoyable encounter where frustration comes from challenge instead of bad luck and tedium.  Similarly you can be in rooms small enough that your attack animation will never finish before an enemy skill which follows you will catch up with you. You solve that by just dodging it until it runs out. However again, due to RNG it is possible for the enemy to immediately follow up with more of that same attack. The dodging itself is not that hard, it's a relatively slow moving projectile compared to your movement, but it's tedious and boring as all get out to dodge it, then dodge another one, then do an attack, then dodge another one, then dodge another one. I could just leave the room, but why make the combat be able to roll into a tedious situation at all? The combat is mostly well designed, why take that away on a dice roll?  This pattern has played out more and more frequently as I progress through the game. It's possible there's some mitigation to it I haven't hit yet, but it really just feels like lazy leaning on RNG that took a game I was loving at the start and is making it progressively less enjoyable as it scales up and opens up more opportunities for dice rolls.

After Investing 16.4 hours into Rogue Legacy 2 and completing approximately 5% of the game, I find myself thoroughly captivated by its engaging progression system. Here's my detailed review, highlighting the pros, cons, and mixed aspects of the game, especially considering its $24.99 price point.  Pros:  1. Addictive Progression Loop: The game masterfully combines roguelite mechanics with persistent upgrades. Each run allows you to collect gold, which can be used to enhance your manor and unlock new classes, abilities, and equipment, providing a tangible sense of advancement even after character deaths.  2. Diverse and Unique Classes: With 15 distinct classes, each offering unique playstyles and abilities, the game ensures that gameplay remains fresh and exciting. Whether you prefer ranged attacks, melee combat, or magic, there's a class tailored to your preferences.  3. Procedurally Generated Environments: The procedurally generated dungeons ensure that no two runs are the same, keeping the exploration aspect intriguing and challenging. This randomness enhances replayability, as you never know what to expect in each run.  4. Charming Art Style and Presentation: The 2.5D art style, featuring 3D characters against hand-drawn backdrops, is visually appealing. The animations are smooth, and the overall aesthetic adds to the game's charm.  Cons:  1. Steep Difficulty Curve: The game can be quite challenging, especially for newcomers to the roguelite genre. Some players might find the initial difficulty spikes discouraging, requiring perseverance to progress.  2. Repetitive Early-Game Grind: Early stages can feel repetitive as you work to gather enough resources to unlock significant upgrades. This grind might deter players seeking immediate variety in gameplay.  Mixed Points:  1. Trait System: The trait system adds both positive and negative modifiers to characters, introducing variety and unpredictability. While it encourages adaptability, some traits can significantly hinder progress, leading to mixed feelings about their impact on gameplay.  2. Narrative Depth: The game offers a rich lore and backstory, but much of it is delivered through collectibles and environmental storytelling. Players seeking a more direct narrative might find this approach less engaging.  Value for Money:  At $24.99, Rogue Legacy 2 offers substantial content and replayability. The combination of diverse classes, procedurally generated environments, and a robust progression system provides excellent value. Considering the depth and quality of the experience, it's a worthwhile investment for fans of the genre.  Conclusion:  Rogue Legacy 2 builds upon its predecessor, delivering a refined and engaging roguelite experience. While it presents challenges, the rewarding progression system and variety in gameplay make it a standout title worth exploring.

This game is trying to be a charming, colourful romp of an action platformer roguelite, with the memorable gimmick of your hero having quirky traits that influence the gameplay on each run.  It's a gimmick that actively works against the fun of the game.  It's possible to get 'lucky' and have a hero whose random trait/class/spell combination is exceptionally powerful; yet it doesn't feel satisfying to use them, because the dungeons are balanced for 'normal' heroes and you're suddenly just basically playing on Easy mode and having game progression handed to you through little merit of your own.  Or, you can pick a hero with a terrible trait like 'only critical hits deal damage'...and have it basically be a dead run. Remember, this is a milestone-based game where you essentially have to redo any section that you fail at, kind of like the bonfire system in Dark Souls but with procedural generation. So unless you're doing it as a challenge run, there's no good reason to pick these negative traits, even with the gold bonus associated with them. Maybe if these traits were actually a good mechanic and a fun/meaningful addition to the game, the developers wouldn't have to bribe you to make you consider trying them.  It's a baffling design choice for a sub-genre that usually focuses on smooth, consistent gameplay. And it's not helped that the dungeon itself gets rather 'same-ish' quite quickly, with not as much enemy and room variety as it probably ideally needs. Also, I'm not a great fan of the enemy designs or map designs, and even some aspects of the UI seem a little clunky to me. Additionally, the relic system feels rather flat, with the effects frequently being meagre, incremental and non-interactive. ("Health drops give an additional 8% of your max health", "Every sixth hit does +75% damage") Almost all of them either feel too plain or too niche.  To its credit, if you're NOT worried about game balance or smooth difficulty or repetition, the game actually has very responsive, crisp controls and decent platforming mechanics. But in this day and age, I'd say that isn't enough on its own. Having lots of classes, weapon variety and 'house rule' gameplay options is certainly nice, and you can tell that the developers were really going for 'Let's make it like the first game, but with more content'.  I just wish they'd stopped to ask themselves whether the content they were adding was actually fun.

Super fun until NG+3 (for reference, the "true ending" can be achieved by NG+7), then the stat % upgrade grind gets way too tedious. For a roguelike, there are not enough meaningful variables during a run to make grinding for hours fun.  Still, it's a clear improvement over Rogue Legacy 1 with a large class variety and fluid movement. Once all abilities are unlocked, I think it's much better in the "movement" part than some other highly rated games like Hollow Knight or Dead Cells.  It's one of those games you come back to every year for a few runs.

I think the achievement of this game is the insane amount of variation that it has.  I feel like a never see a repeat of a randomized room.  The gameplay is incredibly snappy.  The only thing out of place is the writing actually.  You get really serious character backstories in this wacky fun game.

Rogue Legacy looks like a game better played rushing through fights with over-powered equipment, but really, it's better to approach it like you would a souls-like, taking your time in each room and assessing your situation before mashing the attack button, and learning to play each class, as they all play differently to each other.  It's not necessarily easy, but the game does help guide you and if it's still too difficult, you can enable house rules to make it easier, or harder if that's what you want!  It's a great game. I've spent plenty of hours on it, and I expect to put more into it still, as I hope to max out as much as I can!

One of the best gameplay loops I've ever enjoyed in a roguelike game. It's fast paced, easy to pop in for 20 minutes and quit out. I cannot recommend it enough for the gameplay. The story and plot are different.   For a game loaded with cheeky references, silly jokes and humorous abilities, it does try hard to tell a tale of love, loss, and betrayal. But it doesn't land. It's hard to take a journal entry or soul memory talking about self sacrifice serious when as soon as it ends, your character farts with every movement or in the next room over a skeleton named "McRib" is waiting for you.  All that being said, the weapons, abilities, spells, and movement feel incredible. It's the first game since any of the souls series that I've been invested enough to do NG+ runs. As of today finishing NG+8 and getting the true ending, it still makes me want to go back in for more

I have played a lot of Roguelikes, and I think Rogue Legacy 2 is genuinely my favorite of them all. Maybe not the best of them; There's pretty limited build variety beyond class and weapon choice, the end game is pretty lack-luster, run variety isn't that wild either.  But.  It is stuffed to the brim in charm and heart and humor. It feels like home in that sense. I can just pick the game up and play and never have a bad time. The gameplay is so smooth it puts most pure metroidvanias to shame. The classes are ALL fun. All of them. All bangers. The soundtrack is fantastic. The artstyle is cute and charming while also maintaining its own identity. There could definitely be more modifiers and more classes and more zones and more bosses, but I think that's the best flaw a game can have, no? To just want *more* of it.   10/10. Maybe not the perfect roguevania, but for my money its my favorite.

Just to get it out of my system... it's giving Playmobil Dark Souls. LEGO Salt & Sanctuary.  What would happen if Fisher Price took a crack at Blasphemous. The Saturday morning cartoon version of MORK BORG.  What I'm saying is, the silly madcap style is at tremendous odds with the bleak grimpunk story it's trying to tell.  The traitor mourns his betrayal of a dead kingdom sinking into the Stygian waters, seconds before he's chucking wooden spoons at you. A battle-weary Estuary, simultaneously damned by royal duty and crippling paranoia, is felled by a clown chef's poison fart. An heir takes the doomed mantle of their tragically-lost forebear, stopping briefly to chat with a pizza delivery girl and a talking cartoon safe in a life preserver. By the singed beard of your wizard sister, this game's tone is all over the place.  And you know what? Works for me.  The movement mechanics are tight as a drum, the combat is as fair as it is unforgiving, and the sheer variety of classes, weapons, talents, and spells is given further variety by random advantages & disadvantages. The Resolve system adds a spicy bit of risk/reward to runs, forcing you to balance extra edges you may need against the loss of maximum health. From a mechanical gameplay perspective, it is a stand-out in the 2D action platformer realm.  Did you play the first one? Then you pretty much need this one. It's such a noticeable improvement on the first in every way, from the greater variety of options to the improved graphics. Most noticeably, the biomes now actually feel like entirely different places instead of re-skins. My only compliant there is that the 6th Level biome (the underground one) can get tedious with all the double-backing you have to do through cleared rooms to unlock the boss level. But that dragon is so cool, and you can pet it.   Pet the sleepy dragon. That is a 13/10 good dragon.  I've put about 120 hours into the game at time of review and I'm probably gonna put in many more, since I've only gotten to like NG +3 and there's like +7 of those things. I only just unlocked all the basic equipment, and I still have a ways to go upgrading it all. I am STILL unlocking stuff on the Skill Tree. If nothing else, this game delivers value for money.  So yeah, hearty recommend.

i am yet to finish this game in its entirety due to the Incredible total size of the content offered, but this is a title you play for a little and very shortly establish that this is some peoples' life's work and that they [b]bled[/b] for this ♥♥♥♥.  if you had any interest in Rogue Legacy 1 or saw it to the end, this is a very strong recommend. the game is bursting with charm, care, & rock solid balancing decisions; and is one of the only games i can think of where opening up the options menu made me dizzy from just [i]how many[/i] things you could tweak about your experience, with no "by the way, this isn't the Real game :/" stipulations. these are good devs. they make good game.  my only common complaint is an audio mixing one wherein i feel like the music has quite a few good tracks but they are balanced very low in comparison to everything else. even while playing on a SFX/MUS/AMB split of 60/100/60, the music feels muffled or like it's playing in another room in all but boss encounters. this is fine if you have a podcast to listen to but i like Tettix's songs.

前作が好きだったので期待を大きめで始めてみれば、更に期待を超える面白さだったので真エンドまでプレイ。  拠点強化や装備品、ルーンの強化がどんどん進んでいく感覚が非常に楽しい。 天井設定が高すぎて、カンストさせるのは無理だと感じるほどのボリュームがあり、逆にそれがやり込み要素を刺激された。「カンストしたくない」気持ちがうまく満たされるのが最高。  難易度のバランスも素晴らしく、何周しても飽きなかった。 アクション性が高く、敵の種類が豊富で、キャラクターの能力の組み合わせにもランダム性があるので、プレイするたびに新しい発見がある。 ちょっと余裕が出てきたかなと思った瞬間に、あっさり死んでしまう難易度の調整が絶妙で、緊張感が持続された。  特に好きなのは、選んだキャラクターが序盤であっさり死んでも、何もデメリットがなく、むしろ少額でも資産が残るシステム。 自由に失敗できる環境が提供されているので、伸び伸びとプレイできる。 すべての行動がプラスの積分要素になっているゲームは、やっぱり楽しい。  前作の雰囲気を引き継ぎつつも、さらにバージョンアップされている感じがあり、「こういうのでいいんだよ、こういうので」と強く感じた名作！

One of the best rogue-lite games.  Just like in original, you are playing as royal heir, exploring huge family castle with multiple different biomes, trying to kill bosses to progress story or explore to get some gold and open blueprints for new armor and runes.  When you die (and you will die a lot), you gonna play as your heir with gold and materials you collected last run you can upgrade your castle to get permanent upgrades.  Graphics is nice, story and lore are decent.  Gameplay is amazing, with good variety of playable characters and enemies.  Controls are good and customizable for both k+m and controller (I would recommend to play on k+m), game is polished and responsive.  Game is pretty hard, but there are persistent upgrades and a lot of accessibility options to make it manageable (or make it even harder)  Overall Rogue Legacy 2 feels like the original but perfected in every single way. Wholeheartedly recommend for anyone who loves rogue-lite games.

Me pasé el primer juego y este supera muchísimo al primer título en varios aspectos. Es bastante parecido en el sentido del loop jugable, jugar con un descendiente de una de las varias clases que tiene el juego, todas bastante distintas entre si, con modificadores positivos y negativos que varía de personaje a personaje en una dungeon generada aleatoriamente.  Tenemos 5 zonas, cada una de ellas con sus secretos, sus jefes y subjefes. Hicieron más divertida la exploración y el movimiento de los personajes, además añadieron los artefactos que aportan más variedad a las runs, además de poderes que vas desbloqueando a medida que avanzas. El juego te hace sentir constantemente que estás progresando.  Me ha durado 36 horas, de cara al final sobre todo la última zona me cansó un poco por la dificultad. Pero si eres el tipo de jugador que le encanta los retos, los roguelikes y una progresión consecuente este juego es perfecto para ti. Yo no hago new game+ ni desafíos ni retos pero el juego tiene muchísimo contenido y opciones para añadir más dificultad.

An okay game, with some caveats for Metroidvania fans.  1. Be aware that a LOT of player power is gated behind farming. To the level where, if you struggle with a boss, you can just go farm gold for an hour and come back and brute force it. Whether this is a good or a bad thing depends on the player, but it discourages trying to beat a boss with the tools given and being better at it.   2. The difficulty between god and bad runs can be extreme - to the level where I had been struggling for awhile on a boss, and then I got a godlike run and killed 3 bosses in a row with the same character. This can feel a bit awkward.  3. Bosses are one-and-done on your first playthrough, so you will likely spend very little time on bosses compared to the rest of the game. 4. NG+ locks a lot of content, enemies and stuff.. While then encouraging even more grinding.  Took me ~20 hours to complete and I am unlikely to return, but I did enjoy my first playthrough.

Один из лучших рогаликов, в который я играл (1 часть тоже хороша, но уступает). 6 локаций с разными мобами и особенностями. Уникальные механики, пасхалки и просто приятный геймплей. Прекрасный визуал, но проблемы моментами с музыкой. НГ+ добавляет как хардкора так и интереса, открываются усложнения(ноши советую их включать, для большего хардкора): на локациях, на боссах ну и сами мобы становятся сильнее, а также появляются новые. Для открытия истинной концовки нужно 8 НГ+, что уже душновата, но я проходил в своем темпе, и мне все понравилось. Хардкор это то, что нужно в таких играх.  Шрамы: некоторые очень интересны, другие просто хрень на убийство мобов, но я уверен, что это новый виток развития Боссы понравились, но скудный мув-сет, на НГ+ становится интереснее Локации: это постоянно новые проблемы, только приспособился к 1, так тебе кидают другую с новыми особенностями, трудностями и мобами. Все локации очень красивые Особые испытания: вот тут и идет весь хардкор- чистый rogue; танатафобия(и усложненная версия). Здесь игра открывается уже совсем с новой стороны: на чистый rogue нет прокачки, приходится вывозить на знаниях и умениях(удача тоже сильно решает). Советую хотя бы просто попробовать. Сюжет сильно напоминает 1 часть, поколения, царь и другие схожести. Он средненький, но записки читать интересно. Сюжет раскрывается постепенно, но подают его с разных сторон, так что после прохождения на истинную концовку все складывается в цельную картинку. Честно, он растянут, но вряд ли вы зашли ради сюжета Черты персонажа-это то что добавляет интерес к забегу, стать пацифистом, а потом найти оружие и сменить стиль уже на ассасина, 1 хп но взять оружие, которое бьет через стены (катана) и фармить золото.  Под обстоятельства приходится подстраиваться, ведь локации не бесконечные, а предметы с которых можно получить еду ограниченно, приходится где-то жертвовать хп, чтобы получить больше хп; где-то не лутать комнаты, чтобы потом вернуться; что-то скипать и терять золото или предметы; а где-то жертвовать здоровьем, чтобы получить новое снаряжение, артефакт Механики все понятны, остается только оттачивать мастерство и понимание в критических ситуациях. Довольно часто было такое, что остаешься на 1 удар, но делаешь камбек по хп(правда обычно это все равно заканчивается смертью, но 1 раз так даже прошел игру) Музыка местами хороша, но часто ее не хватает, либо она не столь динамичная, shift+tab вам в мощь ставите свой плейлист и идете убивать. Классы-это то что добавляет интерес к игре, у каждого уникальное оружие и способности, которые заставляют игрока подстраиваться и дают новый опыт. Для разных типов игроков подойдет свой класс (мой выбор ронин и обыкновенный рыцарь) Артефакт/реликвии: их тут куча и под разные стили игры. Вы маг, замечательно, вам бафы к урону и восстановление маны, вы безбашенный танк, тогда вам пойдет кольцо возрождение и так далее. За артефакты есть своя цена: 1 их редкость, 2 найти нужное, 3 решимость, если меньше 100%, тогда буде забирать ваше максимальное здоровье  Ачивки: есть как интересные, так и душные, но делается это не так сложно, если вы уже достигли истинной концовки(100% ачивок у меня уже сделаны). Если очень сложно, можно включить свои правила( на последних играх+ или в особых испытаниях. Локализация-это уровень, к которому многим играм нужно стремится Прокачка персонажа стала более разнообразная, нежели в 1 части, что однозначно+. Руны и доспехи: в игре много рун и сетов брони, что открывает большой потенциал для билдов: хочешь будь вампиром, который хилиться с каждым ударом, хочешь будь невероятным магом, а может станешь танком с 100 брони или  убийцей с колоссальным уроном. Так же и с рунами: от фарма денег, до 8 прыжков в воздухе.  Динамика в игре бешеная, визуальные приемы прекрасны  Проблем с отимизацией не замечал, багов также не замечал, когда такое было?)   Реиграбельность одна из лучших в играх данного жанра. Игра не надоедает( по крайней мере мне как любителю рогаликов, отыграв 140 часов могу с уверенность сказать, что нужно брать эту игру, если вы любите этот жанр). Готов поставить 9,5/10. Буду ждать 3 часть (уже фанат этих разрабов). Видно, что игра сделана с душой, и разрабы не зря старались. Сиквел смог не то что переплюнуть 1 часть, а просто оставить ее не у дел. Даже перепроходить ее в 2024 было интересно!     P.S. если вы дочитали, в игре находится 4 пасхальных баклажана, можете чекнуть гайд на ютубе и привести их домой. Im Endeffekt eine verbesserte Version des Ursprungstitels Rogue Legacy. Aus meiner Sicht ist die 2 hier irreführend und keine wirkliche Fortsetzung, eher eine Neuauflage. Wer also noch keinen der beiden Titel gespielt hat, sollte lieber hier zugreifen, da mit diesem Titel im Grunde alles aus dem Vorgänger nochmal aufpoliert bereitgestellt wird. Von der Bewegung, zu den Bonusstats, der Grafik, dem Gegnerverhalten und den Bosskämpfen ist alles verbessert.  Wer den Vorgänger kennt, bekommt hier vom eigentlichen Inhalt dasselbe und wer das gefeiert hat und das in nochmal verbesserter Form erleben möchte, macht hier nichts falsch beim Zugreifen.  Gleichzeitig wird so allerdings jeder enttäuscht sein, der jetzt die Erwartung an den Titel haben sollte, dass hier groß das Rad neu erfunden wurde oder auf einmal eine neue Story oder komplett neuer Inhalt vorhanden ist. Es gibt hier und da von den Eigenschaften der Nachfahren, so wie der Klassen, Waffen, usw. durchaus auch Neues, aber jetzt halt nichts was sich wie ein "neuer Titel" anfühlt.  Ist in etwa vergleichbar mit The Binding of Isaac zu The Binding of Isaac Rebirth, wenn auch nicht ganz so krass vom Wechsel. Im Endeffekt erhält man also einen Rogue Like Dungeon Crawler, der durch die Zufallsauswahl des spielbaren Charakters, deren Waffe, Fähigkeit und diversen Eigenschaften das Spielerlebnis etwas verändert. Während man sich durch das Schloss kämpft ist es auch unvermeidbar, dass man durchaus den ein oder anderen Tod mitnimmt, aber darauf ist es eben auch ausgelegt. Durch Meta Progression verbessert man diverse Stats und schaltet weitere spielbare Klassen frei und schreitet so voran bis man eben die Arealbosse und am Ende den Finalboss legt. Ergänzend zum Vorgänger gibt es hier noch eine schwierigere Variante der Bossfights, die vordefiniert sind und durchaus knackig sein können. Bis man das alles geschafft hat, vergeht etwas Zeit und selbst nach dem finalen Boss kann man noch einige Runden weiterspielen, aber irgendwann reicht es dann auch und das ist auch okay. Bis dahin hat man einige Stunden Spaß gehabt und kann zufrieden weiterziehen. Es gibt hier aus meiner Sicht keinen "unendlichen Wiederspielwert" wie bei einem Binding of Isaac Rebirth.

[i]Rogue legacy 2...[/i]  Плюсы:  -Шикарный саундтрек, который включает в себя и чиловые мелодии, и мощную электрогитару  -Приятный визуальный стиль  -Интересные боссы, особенно улучшенные версии[spoiler]кроме совершенной Ноемы[/spoiler]  -Довольно занимательный и приятный геймплей - во всём улучшенная первая часть)  -Интересный и продуманный лор мира, открывающийся постепенно, раскрывая определённые стороны сюжета и героев.  Из минусов:  -Выпадение снаряжения и рун. Падают крайне редко, за беготню по [u]всей[/u] карте можно собрать от силы 6+- снаряжения и рун, а самого снаряжения столько, что будь здоров, как и рун. Интересный факт: к нг+9 так и не вышло собрать хоть 1 амулет,плащ или шлем +4 уровня, а руна на вампиризм была в количестве одной штуки, как и парочка других...  -Необходимость проходить 100500 раз игру, чтобы открыть усиленную форму каждого босса. Достаточно было сделать 1-2 нг+ и этого бы хватило, но вышло то, что вышло.  -Ужасный баланс нг+. Каждый нг невероятно повышает урон и хп мобам, что те начинают сносить до трети, четверти или больше хп, а прокачка к этому моменту стоит столько, что за забег по [u]всей[/u] карте хватает, в лучшем случае, на десяток уровней, а чистка всей карты - дело не быстрое.  В итоге, можно сказать, что для 1-2х прохождений игра отличная, можно даже сказать шедевральная, но дальше же вытекает ряд минусов, который не очень хорошо сказывается на игровом опыте, из-за чего игра может крайне сильно надоедать и утомлять своей однообразностью и повторением одного и того же, ради получения возможности сразиться новым боссом и частички лора....

J'ai une affection toute particulière pour Cellar Door Games car c'est Rogue Legacy premier du nom qui m'a fait découvrir et aimer les roguelite. Et après avoir poncé Rogue Legacy, j'ai eu la surprise de voir un deuxième titre, et mama...  Exit les graphismes pixel toujours quali mais qui ont malgré tout un peu vieilli et place à un aspect dessin extrêmement beau et travaillé avec une myriade de petits détails dans les animations (exemples : une petite aura bleue au niveau des pieds en cas d'immunité aux pointes ou davantage de frames pour les animations de mouvements des personnages pour les rendre fluides sans altérer le gameplay) qui feront sourire  Changement complet des classes pour donner une identité unique à chaque archétype, et ajout du système de maîtrise (gain d'un bonus par classe qui profite à tous vos personnages) pour encourager à goûter à tout et varier les plaisirs.  Retour de la sacro-sainte lignée familiale avec des traits bons ou mauvais, mais avec cette fois des bonus de gain d'or pour motiver avec des traits handicapants, ainsi que des équipements avec en nouveauté les bonus de set, et un autre gros ajout majeur avec de très nombreux objets qui auront un impact parfois déterminant sur votre run.  Toujours un challenge d'évoluer dans cet univers assez punitif mais tellement jouissif d'apprendre à maîtriser le monde et les classes pour progresser, où chaque pas en avant et chaque nouvelle tentative aller un petit peu plus loin et se rapprocher de la victoire, sans compter le lore trouvable qui donnera des indices pour des éléments cachés.  Chaque environnement est vraiment unique à sa manière et oblige à adapter son gameplay, ce qui rajoute à la diversité de l'ensemble et évite d'avoir l'impression de répéter de trop les mêmes choses.  [b]Les points forts :[/b]  On retrouve l'esprit de Rogue Legacy magnifié avec de nouveaux graphismes et une identité propre à chaque classe proposée. La courbe de difficulté est bien dosée et permettra aux joueurs chevronnés de relever de sérieux défis et aux autres moins expérimentés mais patients de progresser plus lentement sans être non plus mis en face d'obstacles infranchissables. Certains auront la sensation que le jeu ne les aime pas, mais c'est avant tout pour inciter à utiliser toutes les ressources et techniques à disposition pour lui montrer qui est le patron.  [b]Le point faible :[/b] Le grind requis pour les joueurs un peu moins expérimentés pourra en rebuter certains, surtout si l'objectif est avant tout de voir la suite du jeu. Néanmoins des règles personnalisées peuvent être activées pour permettre à ceux rencontrant une trop forte résistance d'ajuster leur expérience de jeu.  Mention toute particulière au studio qui a travaillé en étroite collaboration avec la communauté tout au long de l'accès anticipé pour développer le jeu, écouter les retours et changer certaines choses en conséquence, mais également intégrer des correctifs créés par des membres de la communauté pour fixer certains problèmes, et même aller au-delà de la roadmap pour créer davantage de petit contenu comme des adaptations de décor de saison. Et pour tout ça, merci Cellar Door Games.

This is a great game. Controls are tight, there's a good amount of classes and weapons to keep the game entertaining for a good while and a nice variety of enemies and bosses to dispatch. I loved the first game in the series, so when Rogue Legacy 2 is basically the same, just with more of everything, I'm on board. The difficulty was perfect for me, but if you'd like the game to be easier or harder, you're provided with settings to tailor the experience to suit your preferences. To top it all off, the game has a fun, silly tone to it that I really enjoy. There's some pretty cool lore present, and you're somewhat rewarded for paying attention to it, but it is by no means a requirement. I you just want to beat up some bad guys, you're in good shape to enjoy what this rogue-lite has to offer.

I enjoyed the first Rogue Legacy game a lot, I played it through many times (to New Game +6).  The sequel is better looking, has many more features, and a longer more complex story - the latter being particularly welcome as I had run out of stuff to explore in the original game.  Everything is well executed.  Downsides are that the more complicated controls are at times a bit fiddly, and with so many progression mechanics some of them feel a bit diluted / unnecessary.  The addictive core gameplay is still there though, and there's lots of fun to be had with this game!
